hero.title: 4 Days at the Top of the World.
hero.titleAccent: A Himalayan Village Stay Above the Clouds.
hero.intro: Sleep in a cloud-kissed Himalayan village at 2,200 metres, wake to the full arc of the world's greatest mountain range turning gold, then spend your days in Kathmandu's medieval temples and ancient squares.
hero.heroImageAlt: Nagarkot Himalayan village at dusk Nepal mountain views

excludes: Nepal Visa on Arrival (USD30 for 15 days)
Entrance Fees to temples and sites

overview.heading: 4 Days Nepal Himalayan Villa: The Full Picture
body: Nagarkot is the Himalayan viewpoint that most short Nepal trips either rush through or miss entirely. This package makes it the centrepiece. You stay two nights in a proper Himalayan village lodge, with time to properly experience both the sunset and the sunrise over the full sweep of peaks — a 200-kilometre arc that includes Everest.

Combined with a day in Kathmandu's UNESCO temple complexes, this four-day package offers a genuinely complete experience of Nepal's two most memorable dimensions: ancient civilisation in the valley below and the Himalaya above.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Two Nights in a Himalayan Village
items.body: Nagarkot at 2,200 metres is a true hill village, not a tourist resort. Two nights here means sunset one evening, a full clear morning the next, and enough time to walk the ridgeline paths and simply breathe the mountain air.
items.imageAlt: Nagarkot Himalayan village lodge Nepal sunset

items.title: The 200-Kilometre Himalayan Arc
items.body: On a clear morning from Nagarkot, you see mountain ranges that most people never witness in their lifetimes — Dhaulagiri, Annapurna, Manaslu, Ganesh Himal, Langtang, and on a perfect day, the distant tip of Everest itself.

items.title: Kathmandu's Ancient Temple Circuit
items.body: A full day in Kathmandu's UNESCO World Heritage Sites — the sacred ghats of Pashupatinath, the watching eyes of Boudhanath and the Monkey Temple of Swayambhunath, finished with Durbar Square's medieval pagodas.

days.title: Arrive Kathmandu, Drive to Nagarkot
days.description: Arrive at Tribhuvan International Airport and transfer to your Himalayan village lodge at Nagarkot. Afternoon and evening to settle in and experience the ridge at dusk.
suggestedActivities: Drive to Nagarkot (32 km from Kathmandu)

days.title: Nagarkot Sunrise & Kathmandu Valley
days.description: Rise before dawn for the Himalayan sunrise from your lodge. After breakfast, drive down into the Kathmandu Valley and explore Bhaktapur, the best-preserved medieval city in Nepal.

days.title: Kathmandu Temples & Stupas
days.description: A full day in Kathmandu visiting the three great UNESCO temple complexes — Pashupatinath, Boudhanath and Swayambhunath — followed by Kathmandu Durbar Square.

days.title: Depart Kathmandu
days.description: Free morning in Kathmandu, then transfer to the airport for your onward flight.

whyBook.heading: The Right Lodge Makes Nagarkot
whyBook.body: Not all Nagarkot lodges face the Himalaya. We know which rooms have the view, which mornings are clearest by season, and when to walk the ridgeline. Dhesu has been arranging Himalayan journeys since 1988.

sections.heading: Nagarkot Himalayan Villa Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
Nagarkot is the best Himalayan viewpoint within easy reach of Kathmandu, and two nights here is enough to properly feel the mountains rather than just photograph them from a car window. On a clear October morning, the panorama is one of the most extraordinary sights on earth.

This Nepal tour package from Malaysia combines the cultural heart of Kathmandu with the mountain soul of the high ridgeline — the two things that define Nepal as a destination.

sections.heading: What to Expect at Nagarkot
sections.body:
Nagarkot sits at 2,195 metres on the eastern rim of the Kathmandu Valley. The temperature is noticeably cooler than the city below, and the air is clear. The best Himalayan views stretch from the Annapurna range in the west to Everest, Lhotse and Makalu in the east — a sweep that includes eight of the world's highest peaks.

Clarity is best in October, November, March and April. The lodge stay is comfortable and the ridgeline walks are gentle enough for all fitness levels.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for Nepal?
items.answer: Malaysian passport holders obtain a Visa on Arrival at Kathmandu's Tribhuvan International Airport. A 15-day tourist visa costs USD 30, a 30-day visa costs USD 50. Bring passport photos and the fee in cash. Dhesu provides full guidance with your booking.

items.question: What is the best time to see the Himalayas from Nagarkot?
items.answer: October to December and March to May offer the clearest mountain views. October and November after the monsoon are the very best — crisp air, zero haze and unobstructed peak views. Spring brings rhododendron blooms alongside the mountains. Monsoon months (June–September) often mean cloud cover at the ridge.

items.question: How far is Nagarkot from Kathmandu?
items.answer: Nagarkot is approximately 32 kilometres from central Kathmandu, about a 1.5-hour drive through the Kathmandu Valley and up the ridge road. Your private vehicle and driver handle all transfers throughout the package.

items.question: Can I see Everest from Nagarkot?
items.answer: On a very clear morning (most likely October and November), the summit of Mount Everest is visible from the Nagarkot viewing tower — a tiny pyramid on the far eastern horizon. The Annapurna range, Langtang and Ganesh Himal are reliably visible on clear days and are equally spectacular.

items.question: Can I extend this trip to include Pokhara?
items.answer: Yes. Our 6-day Best of Nepal package adds Pokhara's Phewa Lake and Sarangkot sunrise. We can also build a custom itinerary that combines Nagarkot, Kathmandu and Pokhara to your preferred pace.
